author: tejado
description: Encrypt your notes using GPG. Supports smartcards for enhanced security! Keep your information safe and accessible only to you.

manifest.author: Tjado Mäcke
manifest.description: Seamlessly encrypts your notes using GPG. Supports smartcards for enhanced security! Keep your information safe and accessible only to you.
manifest.name: gpgCrypt

name: gpgCrypt

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
New features:
- Folder encryption (enable it inside Settings)
- Command palette integration for encryption/decryption
releases.name: v0.6.0 - Fodler Encryption + Command palette hotkeys

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
New features:
- New setting to deactivate 'Decryption in progress' dialog
- New setting to enable the reset of the passphrase timeout when an encrypted note is saved
- New setting for compatibility towards Obsidians metadata generation function, that is necessary for features linke link blocks or specific plugins like Excalidraw. It hooks into the Obsidians readBinary function. When enabled, the readBinary function returns the decrypted note. As Obsidian saves the notes metadata on disk, this leaks plaintext headings and the file structure (when compatbility mode is enabled only).
releases.name: v0.5.0 - Usability and compatibility mode settings

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
New features:
- Support OpenPGP.js keys outside of the Vault by specifiying a relative path.

Bugfixes:
- Fix a bug in which pressed keys during the Passphrase modal close caused in certain situations a wrong note-overwrite
releases.name: v0.4.0 - Minor feature update

releases.author: tejado
releases.description:
New features:
- Mobile device support
- "Ask for passphrase on startup" setting for OpenPGP.js backend
- "Decryption cache" setting for GPG CLI backend
- Decryption modal for GPG CLI backend, including the option to cancel the execution of gpg cli
- The last opened encrypted note is now getting loaded again on startup

Bugfixes:
- Fix that multiple passphrase prompts are opened even when the passphrase was correct
- Fix that file-recovery plugin introduced a new event registration that wasnt hooked by gpgCrypt
- Fix to avoid multiple Notices during one note opening
- Minimum passphrase cache time is now 10 seconds
- "Remember passphrase" setting now only available for OpenPGP.js backend as it has no function for GPG CLI backend
- Dependency update
releases.name: v0.3.0 - Feature Update

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Option to encrypt all notes (#1)
- Option to indicate encryption status over "gpg" file extension (#2)
- Improved welcome dialog to be more specific regarding usage of existing keys (#3)
releases.name: v0.2.0 - Feature Update

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Asymmetric encryption with key pair
- Seamless for maximum compatibility
- OpenPGP smartcard support (e.g. with YubiKey, Nitrokey, ...) over gpg CLI Wrapper
- Configurable duration of remembering your password
- Configure how encrypted notes should be handled by the file recovery core plugin.
- Enable/disable compression
- Tested with Obsidian Sync
- Status bar integration
releases.name: Initial release (v0.1.0)
